description: One who gives guidance and advice; a person sought for wisdom. 'Menua' names who helps us see—offering perspective without imposing answers.
sound_symbolism: The word meets with wisdom. 'Me' centers with understanding presence, attention given. 'Nu' nurtures with caring guidance, wisdom shared. Then 'a' opens to received counsel.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo menua' (the counselor). Related to 'thorae' (facilitator) but offers wisdom rather than process. Guides 'thunai' (student).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Counselor as mirror—helping others see themselves more clearly.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Counsel as listening—guidance that begins with deep understanding of the other.
